task: Review the 3 most recently changed route files for code quality: unused imports, SQL injection risks, missing html.escape on user input, hardcoded values that should be CSS variables. Fix anything clear-cut, flag anything ambiguous.

assignments.backend: Identify the 3 most recently changed route files in src/indiestack/routes/ using git log. For each file: (1) find unused imports, (2) audit ALL raw SQL for f-string/format-string injection risks — any user input interpolated into SQL without parameterized queries is a fix, (3) find any user-supplied data (query params, path params, form data) rendered in HTML responses without html.escape(), (4) flag any hardcoded hex colors or pixel values that should use CSS variables. Fix clear-cut issues (unused imports, missing html.escape, parameterized query fixes). Flag ambiguous items with inline comments or a summary.
assignments.frontend: After backend identifies the 3 most recently changed route files, review their HTML template strings for: (1) hardcoded hex colors that should use CSS variables from components.py :root block, (2) hardcoded pixel values for spacing/sizing that break design consistency, (3) any inline styles that duplicate existing CSS variable values. Fix clear-cut replacements, flag ambiguous ones.

decomposition.reasoning: Backend goes first because it needs to identify the 3 files via git log and handle the security-critical items (SQL injection, html.escape, unused imports). Frontend follows to review the same files for design consistency (CSS variables, hardcoded values). Frontend depends on backend to identify which files to review. Strategy & QA is automatic and not included.

strategy_review.reasoning: This is near-identical to two tasks completed less than 30 minutes ago. At 02:01 you ran 'Quick cleanup: find and fix any obvious issues in files changed in the last 3 git commits: typos in user-facing text, missing html.escape calls, hardcoded colors that should use CSS variables' ($2.21). At 02:03 you ran 'Security review: check for SQL injection in any raw f-string queries, missing html.escape on user input, exposed secrets in git-tracked files' ($3.96). Combined cost: $6.17 already spent on the exact same scope — SQL injection, html.escape, hardcoded colors — on recently changed files. The '3 most recently changed route files' are almost certainly the same files those tasks already reviewed and fixed. Running this again is paying $2-4 to re-audit work that was just done. With 53 users and no revenue, that's not defensible.
risk_flags: Direct duplication of two tasks completed in the same session ($6.17 already spent on same scope)
Two-department split adds coordination overhead for work that's already been done
Pattern of running slightly-reworded versions of the same audit — this is the third pass
strategy_review.alternative: Skip this entirely. If there's a specific file or vulnerability the previous two reviews missed, name it and fix that one thing. Otherwise, move on to work that generates revenue or user growth. The codebase was just audited.
